solutions.solution: (d) Refer to solution 7, in option (a), R2 and R5 have English in common. In option (b), R1 and R2 have Japanese in common. In option (c), R1 and R3 have Hindi in common. In option (d), R3 and R4 do not have any language in common.
(b) Refer to solution 7. Three experts know each of Japanese, English and Hindi. Only two experts know French. So, French is least commonly used in the meeting.

(d) Refer to solution 7. R3 knows English and Hindi, while R4 knows French and Japanese. Anyone who knows one language between English and Hindi and one language between French and Japanese can act as an interpreter for R3 and R4. Anyone among R1, R2 and R5 can do this. But answer options say only R1, only R2 and only R5.
(b) Refer to solution 7. If R6 is fluent in Japanese and Hindi, he will be able to communicate with each of R1, R2, R3, R4 and R5.

(c) Order of standing of seven men is given and it is given that G can see all caps except orange. It means that G is wearing orange cap. Now, it is also given that D can see green and blue in front of him but not violet. Also, it is given that E can see violet and yellow. Both these statements combined together tell us that D is wearing violet cap as E can see violet but D cannot. So, D must be wearing violet cap. Now, it is also given that E is wearing indigo coloured cap and from above statements it is clear that A, B and C must be wearing any of the cap from among green, blue and yellow. So, it is clear that F must be wearing red. Arrangement in queue is as follows A → Green, B → Blue, C → Yellow, D → Violet, E → Indigo, F → Red, G → Orange
